exemplar_prompt: Help me draft a short email to my landlord about a leaky tap.
exemplar_text: Hi [name], the kitchen tap has been dripping steadily for the last two days and the drip is getting faster. I've put a cup under it for now but it's filling up overnight. Could you arrange for it to be looked at this week? Happy to be home Tuesday or Thursday after 5pm if that helps. Thanks, [your name]

exemplar_prompt: Explain how vaccines train the immune system, in 3 sentences.
exemplar_text: A vaccine introduces a harmless piece of a pathogen, such as a protein from a virus, so the immune system can study it without being attacked. Specialised cells learn the shape of that piece and produce antibodies that match it. If the real pathogen shows up later, the immune system recognises it immediately and clears it before it can spread.

exemplar_prompt: Recommend a beginner-friendly approach to learning Spanish.
exemplar_text: Start with ten minutes a day of a structured app like Duolingo or Babbel just to get comfortable with the rhythm of the language, then add a single Spanish-language podcast or beginner show that you watch with subtitles. Once those two habits feel automatic after a few weeks, pick up a short graded reader at A1 level and read one page out loud each evening. Consistency matters far more than session length at the start, so keep the daily commitment small enough that you actually do it.

exemplar_prompt: Help me figure out what to cook for dinner with chicken and rice.
exemplar_text: A solid option is a one-pan lemon and garlic chicken with rice. Brown bone-in chicken thighs skin-side down in a hot pan with a little oil, set them aside, then soften a chopped onion and four cloves of garlic in the same fat. Stir in a cup of rice, two cups of stock, the juice of a lemon, salt, pepper, and a sprig of thyme; nestle the chicken back in on top, cover, and cook on low heat for about 25 minutes until the rice is tender and the chicken is cooked through.

exemplar_prompt: What's a polite way to decline an invitation without explaining why?
exemplar_text: The cleanest version is something like 'Thank you so much for thinking of me, but I won't be able to make it this time.' You acknowledge the invite, decline clearly, and stop. You don't owe anyone a reason, and not giving one tends to land more naturally than a fabricated excuse that they might remember next week. If the relationship matters, follow up with a separate, unrelated check-in within the next week so the decline doesn't read as distance.
